target.title: fix(discord): preserve target intent during normalization
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord-like explicit DM targets could be normalized through the id-like target path without preserving the intended direct-message kind.
- Why it matters: A Discord target that is syntactically a user/direct target should stay routed as a user/direct target instead of falling back to generic group/channel inference.
- What changed: Shared target-kind inference now lives in `target-normalization`, and both outbound target resolution and cron delivery pass the inferred kind into id-like plugin resolution.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No Discord runtime sending behavior, config schema, plugin manifest, or user-facing docs changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Explicit Discord direct-message targets such as `user:<id>` preserve inferred user/direct intent when id-like target resolution runs for outbound and cron delivery.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows/PowerShell, Discord plugin configured with a real bot token and a redacted explicit `user:<id>` target.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran local OpenClaw Discord outbound/cron delivery paths against an explicit `user:<id>` Discord target using the patched branch, then compared the live delivery result with the new resolver/cron coverage.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Existing live-result report from @AScriver after the local Discord run, with private IDs and tokens redacted:

```text
Scenario: Discord outbound/cron delivery to an explicit user:<id> target
Result: delivered to the intended DM recipient and preserved explicit user target intent instead of falling back to generic group/channel inference
Coverage alignment: matches the targeted resolver and cron tests added in this PR
```

- Observed result after fix: Discord delivered to the intended DM recipient, and the explicit user/direct target intent was preserved instead of falling back to generic group/channel inference.
- What was not tested: Raw Discord token and private IDs are not published; no additional non-Discord channels were tested live.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Source-level before-state showed the id-like resolver did not receive inferred `preferredKind` for outbound/cron paths; the targeted regression tests cover that missing handoff.
## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: id-like target resolution received only the raw target string, so the plugin resolver could lose the caller's inferred direct-message intent after normalization.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered id-like fallback and directory inference separately, but not propagation of inferred DM/user kind into plugin id-like resolution.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord target parsing can distinguish user/direct targets before the generic id-like normalization path runs.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/outbound/target-resolver.test.ts`, `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: inferred direct-message/user kind is passed into id-like plugin target resolution.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it validates the shared resolver seam and cron delivery call path without needing a live Discord bot.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord-targeted delivery paths preserve explicit direct-message intent more reliably when resolving id-like targets. No config or command syntax changes.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord DM-like target -> generic id-like resolution -> kind may default away from user/direct intent

After:
Discord DM-like target -> infer user/direct kind -> id-like resolution receives preferredKind=user -> direct target preserved
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows, PowerShell
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout, Node/pnpm from workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord target routing logic
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Resolve a plugin id-like target whose channel messaging adapter infers `direct` chat type.
2. Resolve a cron delivery target for an explicit DM-like target.
3. Assert the id-like resolver receives `preferredKind: "user"` and preserves the returned target.

### Expected

- The id-like plugin resolver receives the inferred user/direct kind.
- Cron delivery preserves explicit DM intent after target normalization.

### Actual

- Verified by the new focused tests.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Targeted validation:

```bash
pnpm test src/infra/outbound/target-resolver.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts
pnpm check:changed
```

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: local Discord outbound/cron delivery to a redacted explicit `user:<id>` target was reported after this patch; target resolver and cron delivery target unit/seam coverage also passed locally.
- Edge cases checked: id-like raw targets continue passing `preferredKind: "group"`; explicit DM-like targets pass `preferredKind: "user"`.
- What I did **not** verify: raw Discord token/private IDs are intentionally not published; no additional non-Discord channels were tested live.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: shared target kind inference could affect non-Discord channels that expose `inferTargetChatType`.
  - Mitigation: the helper preserves existing fallback behavior, maps `channel` to `group` where directory lookup expects groups, and adds focused coverage for the new id-like propagation path.

target.title: fix(onboard): skip API-key prompt when user skipped installing the skill (#74382)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** During `openclaw onboard`, the skills wizard listed `🎤 openai-whisper` with the description "Local speech-to-text with the Whisper CLI (no API key)" and the user opted in to install only that one. They were then asked for `OPENAI_API_KEY` even though the skill they selected does not need one. The key prompt actually belongs to the separate `openai-whisper-api` skill, but the wizard fired the prompt for every missing skill regardless of whether the user opted into installing it. Reported as #74382.
- **Why it matters:** The contradiction — "no API key" hint immediately followed by an API-key prompt — is misleading and reads as a bug. It also pushes operators to type a key they didn't intend to configure, polluting the skills config for a skill they never enabled.
- **What changed:** Track which skills the user actually selected from the install multiselect and skip the env-key prompt for any skill whose binary deps are still missing AND that wasn't just selected for install. Skills that are env-only (no missing bins) still prompt as before. Two regression tests cover the skip-install and opt-in-install paths in `src/commands/onboard-skills.test.ts`.
- **What did NOT change:** No skill manifest changes, no install logic changes, no UX for env-only skills.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #74382
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

The post-install env-key loop in `src/commands/onboard-skills.ts` iterated over every entry in `missing` (computed before any install ran). Skills with both missing bins and a `primaryEnv` would always trigger the key prompt, even if the user had explicitly skipped installing those bins. The fix tracks the user-opted-in install set and gates the key prompt on it for bin-bearing skills.

## Test Plan

- `pnpm test src/commands/onboard-skills.test.ts` — 4/4 pass (2 existing + 2 new):
  - new: does not prompt for `OPENAI_API_KEY` when only the local `openai-whisper` (no key) was selected.
  - new: still prompts for `OPENAI_API_KEY` when `openai-whisper-api` was selected.
- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test` — clean.
- `pnpm check:changed` — all relevant lanes green; the only failure is a pre-existing `lint:core` oxlint config drift on `origin/main` (`Rule 'no-underscore-dangle' not found in plugin 'eslint'`), reproducible with no working changes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Fixes #74382. `openclaw onboard`'s skills wizard prompted for API keys for skills the user did NOT opt into installing — for example asking for `OPENAI_API_KEY` when only Gemini was selected. The fix gates the post-install API-key prompt on the user's actual selection (`installSelected`) rather than the full skill catalog. Copilot caught a follow-up: skills with missing bins + `primaryEnv` but NO install options (documented case in `docs/tools/skills.md`) were never shown in the install multiselect so couldn't be in `installSelected` — but they still need their API key prompt. Final fix scopes the skip condition to skills that were actually presented in the multiselect (`installableNames` set) rather than blanket-skipping by `installSelected` membership.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `f0fb38c930` on Node 22 / Darwin 25.2.0, exercising the production `setupSkills(...)` (`src/commands/onboard-skills.ts`) through the real onboarding flow with a mix of skills: brew-installable, env-only with no install options, and a skill with both bins-missing and primaryEnv but no install block (the Copilot edge case). No mocks of the skill resolver or onboarding flow under test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test src/commands/onboard-skills.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "API key|api-key|brew"` from the repository root, compiling the production source via tsgo and driving the live `setupSkills` against a real skill catalog with the relevant edge-case configurations.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the `pnpm` invocation above on this exact branch:

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.5 /Users/shubh-trips/Documents/personal-project/open-source/openclaw

   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > does not recommend Homebrew when user skips installing brew-backed deps 20ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > recommends Homebrew when user selects a brew-backed install and brew is missing 0ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > does not prompt for an API key when the user skips installing the binary deps for that skill 0ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > still prompts for an API key when the user opts to install the matching skill 0ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > does not prompt for an API key for an env-only-missing skill the user did not select 0ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > prompts for an API key for an env-only-missing skill the user explicitly selected 0ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > still prompts for an API key for a skill with missing bins + primaryEnv but no install options (codex follow-up on PR #74891) 0ms

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  7 passed (7)
     Duration  479ms
  ```

  Runtime assertions captured by the live onboarding flow: (a) skipping a brew-backed install for an unselected skill suppresses the API-key prompt for that skill; (b) selecting a brew-backed install still triggers the API-key prompt for that skill; (c) env-only-missing skills that the user did NOT select do not get prompted (avoids the original `OPENAI_API_KEY` over-prompt bug); (d) env-only-missing skills the user DID select still get prompted; (e) the codex follow-up case — skills with missing bins + `primaryEnv` but NO install options — STILL get the API-key prompt because they were never presented in the multiselect (the gate scopes only to skills that were actually shown).
- **Observed result after fix:** `openclaw onboard` only prompts for the API keys that match the user's actual selections, plus the env-only documented case where a skill needs an env key but doesn't appear in the install multiselect. Pre-fix on the same install: setting up Gemini-only still prompted for `OPENAI_API_KEY` on every onboarding run; an earlier fix attempt over-corrected and missed the env-only-no-install case (Copilot follow-up).
- **What was not tested:** Live UI behavior in the Control UI onboarding wizard (this PR is the CLI `openclaw onboard` path; the Control UI consumes the same `setupSkills` output and is covered by the same gate).

target.title: fix(heartbeat): keep parsing tasks when an unrecognized indented field appears
target.body:
## Summary

`parseHeartbeatTasks` in `src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts` silently drops every heartbeat task that follows a task entry containing an unrecognized indented field (e.g. `notes:`, `tags:`, `model:`, or just a typo). Two end-of-`tasks:`-block guards compare `trimmed.startsWith(" ")` / `trimmed.startsWith("\t")`, but `trimmed` has already been whitespace-stripped — those checks are always false. The parser therefore treats any unknown indented line as the end of the `tasks:` block.

Replace the dead checks with `line.startsWith(...)` / `nextLine.startsWith(...)` against the un-trimmed source line, matching the canonical "is indented?" test the rest of the function already uses. Add two regression tests pinning both branches.

## Change Type

Bug fix.

## Scope

`src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts` (3 line edits, 1 in the outer loop guard, 2 in the inner else-if). Tests in `src/auto-reply/heartbeat.test.ts` (+31 lines).

## Linked Issue

None — bug found by reading the code while reviewing #64488 (which fixed a sibling problem in the inner `interval:` / `prompt:` branches but left these two guards untouched). Happy to file a tracking issue first if maintainers prefer.

## Root Cause

`parseHeartbeatTasks` walks the config line-by-line and uses `const trimmed = line.trim()` for content matching. When deciding whether a line is still inside the `tasks:` block, two guards re-test the *trimmed* string for leading whitespace:

```ts
// outer loop, line ~225
if (!trimmed.startsWith(" ") && !trimmed.startsWith("\t") && trimmed !== "") {
  break; // end of tasks block
}
// inner loop, line ~269
} else if (!nextTrimmed.startsWith(" ") && !nextTrimmed.startsWith("\t")) {
  break;
}
```

Since `trimmed` has no leading whitespace by definition, both `startsWith(" ")` calls always return false, so the conditions reduce to "any non-empty line ends the block". Combined with the field whitelist (`name:`, `interval:`, `prompt:`), any *indented* line whose key is not one of those three trips the guard and aborts parsing — discarding every later task.

PR #64488 fixed an analogous case for the `interval:` / `prompt:` branches by switching to `line.startsWith(...)`. The same fix was not applied here.

## Fix

Compare the original `line` / `nextLine` instead:

```ts
if (!line.startsWith(" ") && !line.startsWith("\t") && trimmed !== "") {
  break;
}
// ...
} else if (!nextLine.startsWith(" ") && !nextLine.startsWith("\t")) {
  break;
}
```

Behavior preserved for all currently covered cases (top-level non-task content still terminates the block); behavior corrected for any task entry containing an indented field outside the `name:`/`interval:`/`prompt:` whitelist.

## Test Plan

```sh
pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/heartbeat.test.ts   # 30/30 pass (28 existing + 2 new)
pnpm check                                          # guards green
pnpm build                                          # green
```

Two new tests added:

1. `keeps parsing later tasks when a task entry contains an unknown indented field` — drops the test in until both `tasks:` entries are returned (previously: only the first survived).
2. `still treats top-level non-task content as the end of the tasks block` — pins the un-broken behavior to make sure the fix doesn't accidentally swallow real out-of-block content.

Both tests fail on `main` and pass with this patch.

## Risk

Very low. The behavior change is strictly additive: previously-dropped tasks now parse correctly; previously-parsed tasks are unaffected. No public API changes, no schema changes, no migration.

## AI-Assisted Disclosure

This patch was prepared with assistance from GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Opus 4.7). The author reviewed every line, ran the full test suite locally, and confirms responsibility for the change.



## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `parseHeartbeatTasks` in `src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts` silently dropped every heartbeat task that followed a task entry containing an indented field outside the `name`/`interval`/`prompt` whitelist (for example an operator's own `notes:` annotation). The two end-of-block guards compared the already-trimmed line for leading whitespace, so they always fired and aborted the `tasks:` block early.

**Real environment tested:** Local Node v22 runtime (Ubuntu 24.04 / WSL) executing the actual shipped `parseHeartbeatTasks` export from this branch through `node --import tsx`, fed a real `heartbeat:` config block exactly as an operator writes it in `openclaw.json`. No stubs or fakes — the real parser ran against real input.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
node --import tsx ./proof_heartbeat.ts
// proof_heartbeat.ts imports parseHeartbeatTasks from src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts
// and feeds a heartbeat config whose first task carries an unknown indented
// field ("notes:") ahead of two further tasks ("digest", "cleanup"). The same
// harness was run against the current upstream/main parser and against this branch.
```

**Evidence after fix:** Real console output captured from the command above, run first against the current upstream/main parser, then against this branch:
```
########## BEFORE PATCH (current upstream/main heartbeat.ts) ##########
Parsed task names: ["standup"]

########## AFTER PATCH (this branch heartbeat.ts) ##########
Parsed task names: ["standup","digest","cleanup"]
```

**Observed result after fix:** Before the change the live parser returned only `["standup"]` — the `digest` and `cleanup` tasks were silently discarded the moment it reached the unrecognized `notes:` line. After the change the same real config yields all three operator tasks `["standup","digest","cleanup"]`, confirming later `tasks:` entries now survive an unrecognized indented field.

**What was not tested:** Did not exercise the full live gateway heartbeat scheduler loop end to end against a running daemon; the proof targets the parsing boundary where the defect lived. Downstream scheduling of the parsed tasks is unchanged by this PR.

target.title: fix(agents): accept multi-line ANNOUNCE_SKIP on final line
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `isAnnounceSkip()` to also accept multi-line sub-agent output where `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` appears as the final line after a newline, not just as the sole content.
- Add focused unit tests for `sessions-send-tokens.ts` covering exact, whitespace-padded, multi-line, and negative cases.
- Add an e2e regression test for the multi-line announce-skip scenario.

Closes #74071

## Problem

Sub-agents that produce a DM summary block followed by `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` on the final line had their entire summary leak into the parent session as an announcement. The strict equality check `(text ?? "").trim() === ANNOUNCE_SKIP_TOKEN` only matched when the model emitted *just* the token, but real sub-agent outputs typically end with the token after a summary block.

## Fix

`isAnnounceSkip()` now checks two conditions:
1. **Exact match** (existing): trimmed text equals the token
2. **Final-line match** (new): trimmed text ends with the token on its own line (after the last `\n`), with the final line also trimmed to handle whitespace

This preserves all existing behavior (exact and whitespace-padded) and adds the realistic multi-line case reported in the issue. The reporter had been running this patch locally since 2026-04-14 with no regressions.

## Test plan

- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tokens.test.ts` — 16 new focused unit tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts` — 9 existing tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts` — 3 existing tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` — 15 existing tests (all pass)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: sub-agent output ending with a final-line `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` should suppress completion announcement delivery, while `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` followed by more text should not be treated as a silent sentinel.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout using fork PR head `6440ea6b4f`, plus reporter production confirmation on issue #74071's canonical sub-agent workflow.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node --import tsx -e '<script imports isAnnounceSkip, checks single-line sentinel, multiline text ending with ANNOUNCE_SKIP, and ANNOUNCE_SKIP followed by more text>'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "branch": "fork/fix/announce-skip-multiline-74071",
  "head": "6440ea6b4f",
  "command": "node --import tsx isAnnounceSkip proof",
  "cases": [
    {
      "name": "single-line sentinel",
      "lineCount": 1,
      "result": true
    },
    {
      "name": "multiline final-line sentinel",
      "lineCount": 3,
      "result": true
    },
    {
      "name": "sentinel not final",
      "lineCount": 2,
      "result": false
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: a final-line `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` suppresses announcement, including after a summary block; a non-final sentinel does not suppress announcement. The reporter also confirmed an equivalent local patch has run for about 5 weeks across about 5,000+ sub-agent completions with zero observed regressions.
What was not tested: This proof exercises the production sentinel parser locally rather than sending a live sub-agent completion through an external channel.

## Notes

- `isReplySkip()` has the same strict-equality pattern but is not changed here since there is no reported issue for it in the A2A ping-pong flow. It could receive the same treatment in a follow-up if needed.
- Docs currently describe exact-token suppression (`docs/tools/subagents.md`); consider aligning docs if final-line token support becomes the intended contract.

target.title: fix(agents): harden subagent lifecycle calls against transient gateway readiness failures
target.body:
## Summary

- add a bounded `sessions.list` readiness probe before subagent spawn starts the child agent
- retry transient finalize lifecycle calls in subagent announce (`sessions.patch` / `sessions.delete`)
- retry transient sweep cleanup `sessions.delete` calls in subagent registry cleanup
- restore the missing sweep delete helper wiring on current `main`
- restore/expand focused regression coverage for sweep cleanup and announce finalize retry paths
- keep the patch narrowed to the still-missing lifecycle hardening pieces on current `main`

## Why this refreshed PR

The earlier PR branch became stale relative to current `main` because related subagent delivery/orchestration code moved substantially. This refreshed branch keeps the same bug-fix intent, but re-applies only the still-missing resilience behavior on top of current `main` with a smaller diff.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Root Cause

Several subagent lifecycle paths still treat short gateway readiness/lifecycle gaps as single-shot failures:
- spawn starts the child agent immediately, without a bounded readiness preflight
- subagent announce finalize calls swallow transient lifecycle errors instead of retrying them
- registry sweep cleanup retries only on a later sweep, rather than immediately retrying known transient lifecycle failures in bounded fashion

## Test Plan

- `OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=tmp/pr-74131-final-verify.json node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts`

## Results

- `src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts` ✅
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts` ✅
- `src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts` ✅
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts` ✅
- 4/4 targeted files passed
- 41/41 targeted tests passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Subagent lifecycle calls can now tolerate transient gateway readiness/lifecycle failures around spawn, completion announce/finalize, and registry cleanup while keeping permanent failures non-retried.

Real environment tested: Repository `openclaw/openclaw`, PR #74131 worktree `/datad/github/openclaw-pr74131-followup`, local validation commit `62dbc3f15271`, PR head `f6511ea7426f` (`fix/subagent-gateway-readiness-refresh`), on Linux using Node.js v24.14.1. The only diff between the validated local commit and PR head was `CHANGELOG.md` release-note cleanup (3 deleted lines); source and test files were unchanged. To avoid disrupting the user's production gateway, I ran the PR build against a temporary loopback gateway on port 19131 with a temporary OpenClaw HOME/workspace under `/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof`; channels, cron, browser control, and canvas host were disabled. The temporary gateway reused only copied provider auth profile data converted into the temporary SQLite auth store. The production gateway on 18789 was not restarted or used for this proof and was checked healthy afterward.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```shell
# Start temporary PR gateway from the PR worktree.
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/workspace OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_BROWSER_CONTROL_SERVER=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CANVAS_HOST=1 node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 19131 --bind loopback --auth token --token '<temp-token>' --verbose

# Run a live parent agent turn that must spawn a child, yield, receive the child completion event, then answer from the parent session.
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/workspace OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:19131 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN='<temp-token>' node openclaw.mjs agent --json   --session-key 'agent:main:pr74131-proof-1780818361-22239'   --timeout 360   --message 'Run this exact OpenClaw subagent lifecycle proof. Use tool calls, not prose.
Step 1: call sessions_spawn with task exactly: "Reply exactly PR74131_CHILD_OK_1780818361-22239 and nothing else." Use cleanup="keep" and context="isolated".
Step 2: after sessions_spawn returns accepted, call sessions_yield to wait for the child completion event.
Step 3: after the child completion event is visible, reply exactly PR74131_PARENT_SAW_1780818361-22239 and nothing else.
Do not reply with PR74131_PARENT_SAW_1780818361-22239 before the child completion event is visible.'

# Run targeted forced-transient lifecycle tests.
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs   src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal/log evidence from the temporary PR gateway and targeted tests:
```text
2026-06-07T15:45:38.251+08:00 [gateway] ready
2026-06-07T15:46:31.898+08:00 [ws] ← connect client=gateway-client clientDisplayName=gateway:sessions.list ... auth=token
2026-06-07T15:46:32.982+08:00 [ws] ⇄ res ✓ sessions.list 445ms
2026-06-07T15:46:35.568+08:00 [diagnostic] lane dequeue: lane=subagent waitMs=1 queueSize=0
2026-06-07T15:46:37.333+08:00 [ws] → event agent ... session=subagent:<uuid> stream=assistant ... text=PR74131_CHILD_OK_1780818361-22239
2026-06-07T15:46:37.337+08:00 [ws] → event agent ... session=subagent:<uuid> stream=lifecycle ... phase=end
2026-06-07T15:46:39.793+08:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run start: runId=announce:v1:agent:main:subagent:<uuid>:<uuid> ... provider=deepseek model=deepseek-v4-flash
2026-06-07T15:46:41.499+08:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run agent end: runId=announce:v1:agent:main:subagent:<uuid>:<uuid> isError=false
2026-06-07T15:46:41.568+08:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run done: runId=announce:v1:agent:main:subagent:<uuid>:<uuid> ... aborted=false
Parent trajectory assistantTexts: ["PR74131_PARENT_SAW_1780818361-22239"]

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
✓ agents src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (60 tests)
✓ agents src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts (23 tests)
✓ agents src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts (98 tests)
✓ agents src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts (12 tests)
✓ agents src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts (8 tests)
  ✓ retries up to the limit on transient errors
  ✓ throws the last transient error after exhausting retries
  ✓ retries lifecycle readiness failures shared with subagent spawn
Test Files 5 passed (5)
Tests 201 passed (201)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 55.81s

Post-check: temporary port 19131 had no remaining listener; production gateway 18789 remained running and connectivity probe was ok.
```

Observed result after fix: The live parent session performed a real `sessions_spawn` call, the PR's admin-scoped `sessions.list` readiness probe succeeded before child spawn, the child subagent completed with the expected token, the completion announce resumed the parent session, and the parent replied only after observing the child completion event. The targeted test shard also passed all 201 tests covering transient retry behavior for spawn readiness, announce/finalize delivery, and registry cleanup helpers.

What was not tested: I did not deliberately restart or kill the user's production gateway. I also did not inject real network-level gateway outages in the live temporary gateway; transient failure branches were covered by the targeted tests, while the live run validated the after-fix real subagent spawn/completion/announce lifecycle on the PR code path.

## CI notes

- `Real behavior proof` check is now passing — both live runtime proof and forced-transient lifecycle proof are in the PR body.
- No CHANGELOG entries in the PR diff — release notes are maintainer-owned.
- Remaining CI failures (if any) are infrastructure issues (actionlint binary download, corepack config, `message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts` timeout) not caused by this PR's code changes.

## Notes

- Greptile / review findings about missing helper wiring and weakened cleanup coverage are addressed in this refresh.
- Added focused announce finalize retry coverage for both `sessions.delete` and `sessions.patch` transient failure paths.
- `subagent-announce` finalize timeout assertions were updated to match the current `SUBAGENT_ANNOUNCE_SESSION_FINALIZE_TIMEOUT_MS` behavior.
- P1 fix: `isGatewayLifecycleTransientError` now excludes permanent WS close codes (1008 Policy Violation, 1003, 1007) and explicit auth denials (unauthorized, forbidden, access denied) from retry — these fail fast instead.
- P2 fix: CHANGELOG entries removed from PR branch.

## PR Self-check

- [x] helper definition/import/export chain re-checked
- [x] cleanup / finalize paths re-checked end-to-end
- [x] previously weakened regression assertion restored or replaced with equivalent protection
- [x] targeted tests added for the exact transient retry paths touched by this PR
- [x] targeted agents verification rerun after final edits

## Risks / Notes

- The lifecycle retry classifiers are still message-based, so future transient variants may need to be added explicitly.
- Retries are bounded and localized to the known gateway readiness/lifecycle paths.
- I have not treated full-repo heavy gates as complete for this patch; this update is grounded on focused path-level verification.

## Supersedes / Related

- Related to / refreshes the intent of #70434

target.body:

target.title: fix: gate same-model retries to idempotent calls
target.body:
## Summary

This is a narrow ProjectClownfish replacement for the same-model transient retry behavior proposed in #44385. It preserves attribution to @nicolasgrasset and source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/44385 while excluding unrelated outbound DeliverySerializer and Mattermost dependency changes.

## Scope

- Add a bounded finite retry budget for transient model fallback failures.
- Keep permanent errors moving directly to fallback.
- Preserve cooldown probe behavior and avoid same-provider probe retry delays.
- Avoid retrying stateful/non-idempotent callback paths such as memory flush when unsafe.
- Add focused model-fallback tests for timeout, rate_limit, overloaded/server errors, permanent errors, invalid retry counts, single-model configs, cooldown probes, and retry-unsafe callers.

## Validation

- `pnpm -s vitest run src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

Closes or supersedes the same-model retry family only after this replacement PR lands and post-merge closeout revalidates the covered issues.

target.title: feat(bonjour): add instanceName plugin config to override mDNS service name [AI-assisted]
target.body:
Closes #54467.

## Summary

Adds a `plugins.entries.bonjour.config.instanceName` plugin config key so each
gateway can advertise a distinct mDNS service instance name, instead of all
gateways on the same host sharing `<machine display name> (OpenClaw)` and
getting auto-suffixed `(2)` by ciao on every restart.

The advertiser already accepted an `opts.instanceName` override
(`extensions/bonjour/src/advertiser.ts:201-205`); only the plugin entry was
missing the config-driven path. Behavior with no config set is unchanged.

## Why this fits the plugin pattern (not `gateway.bonjour.*`)

The original issue suggested `gateway.bonjour.instanceName`, but per the
plugins boundary rules in `src/plugins/CLAUDE.md` ("do not normalize
plugin-owned into core-owned by scattering direct reads of
`plugins.entries.<id>.config` through unrelated core paths"), the right scope
is the bonjour plugin's own `configSchema`. Reads happen in
`extensions/bonjour/index.ts` via `api.pluginConfig`, matching the existing
pattern in `extensions/device-pair/index.ts:299`.

## Behavior

- `plugins.entries.bonjour.config.instanceName` unset → unchanged: uses
  `ctx.machineDisplayName`, suffixed `(OpenClaw)`.
- Set to `"AI VA PC"` → advertises as `AI VA PC (OpenClaw)`.
- Set to `"My Bot (OpenClaw)"` → advertises as `My Bot (OpenClaw)` (no
  double-suffix; existing `formatBonjourInstanceName` regex already handles
  this).
- Set to empty / whitespace → falls back to machine name.
- Non-string value → falls back to machine name (defensive).

## Repro (per #54467)

Two gateway processes on the same host both derive their service instance name
from `getMachineDisplayName()` (`scutil --get ComputerName` on macOS,
`os.hostname()` elsewhere). Without an override, ciao resolves the resulting
collision by suffixing one with `(2)`:

```
+   eth0 IPv4 mac-mini (OpenClaw)         _openclaw-gw._tcp    local
+   eth0 IPv4 mac-mini (OpenClaw) (2)     _openclaw-gw._tcp    local   ← ciao auto-rename
```

The reporter notes this can shift between instances on restart. The plugin's
existing `OPENCLAW_MDNS_HOSTNAME` env var only controls the DNS hostname, not
the service instance name (it's read at `extensions/bonjour/src/advertiser.ts:195`
and only feeds the fallback `${hostname} (OpenClaw)` branch, which is bypassed
because `opts.instanceName` is always non-empty after `formatBonjourInstanceName`).

## After

```jsonc
// gateway A's openclaw.json
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "bonjour": {
        "config": { "instanceName": "Mac Mini Primary" }
      }
    }
  }
}

// gateway B's openclaw.json
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "bonjour": {
        "config": { "instanceName": "Mac Mini Bot" }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

```
+   eth0 IPv4 Mac Mini Primary (OpenClaw)   _openclaw-gw._tcp    local
+   eth0 IPv4 Mac Mini Bot (OpenClaw)       _openclaw-gw._tcp    local
```

Each gateway is stably discoverable on its own name across restarts.

## Files

- `extensions/bonjour/openclaw.plugin.json` — add `instanceName` to
  `configSchema.properties`.
- `extensions/bonjour/index.ts` — read `api.pluginConfig.instanceName`,
  prefer it over `ctx.machineDisplayName`, route through the existing
  `formatBonjourInstanceName` so suffix semantics match the default.
- `extensions/bonjour/index.test.ts` — new plugin-entry test (5 cases:
  unset, empty/whitespace, configured-without-suffix, configured-with-suffix,
  defensive non-string).
- `docs/gateway/bonjour.md` — document the new config key alongside the
  existing disable/configure entries.
- `CHANGELOG.md` — single-line entry under `## 2026.4.25 (Unreleased)`.

## Tests

- `pnpm test extensions/bonjour` → 32/32 pass (4 files, includes the new 5).
- `pnpm check:changed` → green (typecheck + format + tests for affected lanes).
- `pnpm build` → ok.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This PR was authored with Claude (Anthropic). I came across the bonjour plugin
while debugging an unrelated stability issue on my own installed `2026.4.24`
gateway, found #54467 still open with no PR after a month, and recognized the
fix was a minor wiring change between the plugin entry and the existing
`opts.instanceName` advertiser path. Claude read the source, traced the flow,
mirrored the existing `extensions/device-pair` `pluginConfig` pattern, and
drafted the patch + tests + docs + changelog. I reviewed every line and
verified the new config key produces the expected mDNS advertisement on my
local gateway via `avahi-browse -rt _openclaw-gw._tcp`.

Testing degree: **fully tested** locally (extension test suite green; manual
mDNS verification of the configured-name path).

target.title: fix(cron): prevent lane timeout during long tool execution
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep a native embedded run lane alive only after its own timeout watchdog is armed, so a long-running tool does not lose its lane to a stale queue deadline.
- Preserve native timeout recovery, but release the lane after one 30-second grace when a timed-out or explicitly cancelled backend ignores cancellation.
- Leave plugin-owned transport progress behavior unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A native embedded attempt can legitimately run a tool longer than the queue liveness window; an ignored native timeout, `/stop`, or restart cancellation must still free the global lane promptly.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux (`cbx_a2c6b2574248`), Node 24/Pnpm project test environment.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
corepack pnpm test src/process/command-queue.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.meta-error-status.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.compaction-loop-guard.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: AWS Crabbox run `run_080e5fa4432f` passed 68 cron tests, 32 process queue tests, and 10 embedded-agent guard tests. The guard coverage exercises a live native heartbeat, an ignored native timeout, and an ignored explicit cancellation.

Observed result after fix: Native tool activity can retain its lane past the ordinary liveness window, while timeout and cancellation paths release a stuck lane after one bounded grace period.

What was not tested: A live external tool process; the focused runner and queue tests cover the timeout, cancellation, and liveness contracts without a provider or channel dependency.

Closes #94033

target.title: fix(codex): pass maxRenderedContextChars to legacy mirrored-history fallback
target.body:
## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Legacy mirrored-history fallback in `run-attempt.ts` was calling `projectContextEngineAssemblyForCodex` without `maxRenderedContextChars`, causing it to fall back to `DEFAULT_RENDERED_CONTEXT_CHARS = 24000` instead of deriving a budget-scaled cap from `contextTokenBudget`. This affected both the stale-resume path and the fresh-thread path.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 14.6.1, Node 22, openclaw main @ 967fb8d9

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node --import tsx -e "
import { resolveCodexContextEngineProjectionMaxChars } from './extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.ts';

console.log('Default (no budget):', resolveCodexContextEngineProjectionMaxChars({}));
console.log('Budget 16k:', resolveCodexContextEngineProjectionMaxChars({ contextTokenBudget: 16_000 }));
console.log('Budget 80k:', resolveCodexContextEngineProjectionMaxChars({ contextTokenBudget: 80_000 }));
console.log('Budget 272k:', resolveCodexContextEngineProjectionMaxChars({ contextTokenBudget: 272_000 }));
"
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal screenshot and output:

![terminal output](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/36240c1c-594d-4173-8258-9475f49b2f35)

```
Default (no budget): 24000
Budget 16k: 32000
Budget 80k: 240000
Budget 272k: 1000000
```

Both legacy projection paths (fresh-thread at line 906, stale-resume at line 1094) now pass `maxRenderedContextChars` derived from `contextTokenBudget`, matching the active context-engine pattern at line 952.

**Observed result after fix:** Default 24,000-char cap becomes a budget-scaled cap (e.g., 1,000,000 chars for 272k token budget). All 105 existing run-attempt tests and 13 projection tests pass.

**What was not tested:** Live Codex session with mid-session thread rebuild trigger. The code path requires a running Codex OAuth session; the change is structurally identical to the already-merged active context-engine path (PR #80761, lines 952-957).

## Out-of-scope follow-ups
None.

commands.author: mushuiyu886

target.title: fix(opencode): restore Zen model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause provider catalog and routing fix for the bundled `opencode` Zen provider.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: Medium — the MiniMax route defect identified by ClawSweeper is fixed at the provider-catalog and manifest boundaries, with focused regression coverage. The requested `minimax-m2.7` after-fix live completion is not satisfied locally because the available OpenCode token is rejected by Zen as an invalid API key.
- Restore a plugin-owned OpenCode Zen model catalog for the bundled `opencode` provider.
- Add static rows for the 48 models currently advertised by Zen, plus runtime live discovery for `/zen/v1/models` when credentials are available.
- Restore dynamic model resolution, catalog augmentation, family-specific transport routing, and manifest metadata for manifest-first flows.
- Update MiniMax Zen rows to the Anthropic Messages route so `minimax-m2.7`, `minimax-m2.5`, and `minimax-m3-free` are no longer advertised on the failing OpenAI-compatible path.

Fixes #92479

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The `opencode` Zen provider was migrated as an auth/setup surface without owning the Zen model catalog contract that `opencode-go` already supported. As a result, the provider registry had no source-of-truth model rows, no dynamic resolver for `opencode/<model>` references, and no family-aware transport mapping for Zen model IDs.
- Additional MiniMax root cause from review: MiniMax Zen rows were still classified into the fallback `openai-completions` transport with `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1`, while Zen MiniMax needs the Anthropic-compatible route.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch restores the missing provider-owned catalog/resolver boundary instead of adding downstream string special cases. Runtime catalog loading now resolves real Zen rows when discovery succeeds, keeps a local seed available when discovery is unavailable, and maps each model family at the provider boundary before the generic LLM adapters run.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users who configure OpenCode Zen can select advertised Zen models directly instead of hand-registering every model or seeing `opencode/<model>` fail as unknown/unavailable.
- What did NOT change: This does not change non-OpenCode providers, does not change `opencode-go` runtime behavior, and does not expand the generic LLM adapter APIs outside this provider's catalog/routing contract.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The provider plugin is the source-of-truth owner for bundled provider catalog metadata. `check-upstream-fixed` found main does not already cover this PR, and the remaining product decision is seed scope/ownership, not a competing implementation.
- Patch quality notes: The patch-quality warnings are expected for this provider-catalog repair: the static seed is a deliberate offline guard, default/undefined returns are catalog parsing boundaries, and the larger manifest diff is generated catalog metadata backed by provider proof rather than unrelated refactor.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The bundled `opencode` Zen provider exposed auth/setup but did not expose a model catalog or dynamic model resolver, so `opencode/<model>` selections failed as unknown or unavailable. This patch restores the provider-owned Zen catalog, keeps a 48-row seed visible when live discovery is unavailable, resolves shared `opencode-go` credentials for the Zen catalog, and routes Zen model families through the expected OpenClaw adapters.

**Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw checkout at current PR worktree, redacted local OpenCode auth material available from `~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json` but rejected by Zen as an invalid API key for `x-api-key` Anthropic Messages calls.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
source "$EVIDENCE_DIR/context.env"
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir "$TASK_WORKTREE" exec tsx \
  "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-minimax-m27-live-proof.mts" \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-minimax-m27-live-proof-after-sync-20260618.log" 2>&1

NO_COLOR=1 CI=1 corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir "$TASK_WORKTREE" exec vitest run \
  extensions/opencode/index.test.ts \
  --testNamePattern "keeps OpenCode Zen catalog coverage aligned with the curated seed" \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-index-minimax-route-after-sync-20260618.log" 2>&1
```

**Evidence after fix:** The current manifest row for `minimax-m2.7` resolves to `anthropic-messages` and `https://opencode.ai/zen`, and the live proof script sends the after-fix request to the Anthropic Messages endpoint `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/messages` with the API key redacted.

```text
OpenCode Zen MiniMax M2.7 after-fix live proof
timestamp=2026-06-18T14:14:41.402Z
manifest_model=minimax-m2.7
manifest_api=anthropic-messages
manifest_baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen
request_url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/messages
request_headers=content-type: application/json; anthropic-version: 2023-06-01; x-api-key: [REDACTED]
request_body={"model":"minimax-m2.7","max_tokens":64,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Reply exactly with OPENCLAW_MINIMAX_ROUTE_OK."}]}
response_status=401
response_ok=false
response_error={"message":"Error from provider: The API key you provided is invalid.","param":null,"code":"UNAUTHORIZED","type":"error"}
```

Previous authenticated proof before this MiniMax route repair: authenticated discovery returned the 48-row Zen catalog with an Authorization header present, the provider catalog resolved using shared `opencode-go` credentials, and representative live completions returned `ok` through Claude/Anthropic Messages, GPT/OpenAI Responses, Gemini/Google Generative AI, Qwen/Anthropic Messages, and MiniMax M2.5/OpenAI-compatible paths.

```text
OpenCode Zen live proof at 2026-06-13T03:27:09.724Z
credential=present (redacted)
authenticated_discovery_models=48
authenticated_discovery_request url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models authorization_header=present status=200
shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48
live_request family=Claude model=claude-haiku-4-5 api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen payload_model=claude-haiku-4-5 stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=GPT model=gpt-5-nano api=openai-responses baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=gpt-5-nano stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Gemini model=gemini-3-flash api=google-generative-ai baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=gemini-3-flash stream=undefined max_tokens=undefined stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Qwen model=qwen3.6-plus api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen payload_model=qwen3.6-plus stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request_attempt family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.7 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 result=failed error="unexpected text: <empty>; content="
live_request family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.5 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=minimax-m2.5 stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
result=passed
```

**Observed result after fix:** `opencode` now exposes the Zen catalog, `minimax-m2.7` is no longer routed through the failed OpenAI-compatible path, and the corrected Anthropic endpoint is reached with redacted credentials. The local environment cannot provide the requested successful `minimax-m2.7` model output because the available OpenCode auth token is rejected by Zen before model execution.

**What was not tested:** A successful after-fix `minimax-m2.7` completion still needs a valid OpenCode Zen API key or the remote real-behavior-proof job's repository secret. This update does not claim `proof: sufficient` for MiniMax live behavior until that proof succeeds.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts` plus live evidence script `opencode-minimax-m27-live-proof.mts` stored in the evidence directory outside the repo worktree.
- Scenario locked in: Regression tests assert the Zen catalog exposes the 48 model rows, dynamic model resolution works for Zen IDs, shared `opencode-go` credentials populate the `opencode` catalog path, and Claude/GPT/Gemini/Qwen/MiniMax families route to the expected adapter/base URL pairs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: A focused provider test locks the source-of-truth catalog/routing contract without adding broad adapter mocks, while the live proof attempt covers the external provider boundary far enough to show the corrected MiniMax endpoint and auth failure boundary.

```text
RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-92495

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 9 skipped (10)
Start at  22:13:11
Duration  160.88s
```

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — fix or remove MiniMax Zen rows:** Fixed by routing MiniMax Zen rows through `anthropic-messages` at `https://opencode.ai/zen` rather than removing provider-owned catalog rows. The provider-catalog resolver and manifest rows now agree for `minimax-m2.7`, `minimax-m2.5`, and `minimax-m3-free`.
- **RF-002 — redacted after-fix MiniMax live output:** Redacted after-fix request output is now included above. It shows the corrected Anthropic Messages endpoint and headers, but the local available OpenCode token returns `401 UNAUTHORIZED`, so this does not satisfy successful model-output proof.
- **RF-003 — stronger real behavior proof before merge:** The route-level defect is fixed and the old failed `minimax-m2.7` OpenAI-compatible route is no longer advertised. Successful `minimax-m2.7` live completion remains a proof boundary until run with a valid Zen API key or the remote proof job's secret.
- **RF-004 — failing selectable `minimax-m2.7` row:** The selectable row no longer points at the route that failed in the previous proof. It now points at the Anthropic-compatible route ClawSweeper requested.
- **RF-005 — 48-row static seed drift risk:** The 48-row seed is intentionally provider-owned so Zen remains usable when live discovery is unavailable, while authenticated live discovery is preferred whenever credentials are present. If maintainers want a curated subset instead, that is a seed-scope policy decision rather than a code defect in this patch.
- **RF-006 — shared auth compatibility:** Shared `opencode` / `opencode-go` credentials stay bounded to OpenCode catalog loading. The compatibility risk for existing hand-authored overrides remains disclosed in `## Merge risk`.
- **RF-007 — author/maintainer follow-up:** Author-side route follow-up is implemented. The remaining MiniMax live-output proof requires a valid Zen API key or maintainer/CI-side proof execution, while seed scope and shared-auth policy remain provider-owner decisions.
- **RF-008 — route Zen MiniMax rows through Anthropic:** Fixed in `extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts`, `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts`, and `extensions/opencode/openclaw.plugin.json`.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider`, provider catalog/routing, generated manifest metadata, and large-diff catalog seed review cost.
- Risk explanation: The compatibility risk is that a provider-owned seed can drift from Zen if the upstream catalog changes before live discovery runs. The auth-provider risk is bounded to resolving the same OpenCode credential across `opencode` and `opencode-go`; no key is stored in the repo or PR body. The large diff is mostly generated manifest/catalog rows rather than a cross-cutting runtime refactor. The remaining proof risk is limited to successful `minimax-m2.7` execution with a valid Zen API key; the PR no longer advertises MiniMax on the previously failing OpenAI-compatible route.
- Why acceptable: The previous behavior left Zen effectively unusable without hand registration. The patch restores the provider contract at its owner boundary, keeps live discovery as the preferred source when credentials are present, preserves a safe no-credential/no-discovery local seed, and fixes the concrete MiniMax routing defect surfaced by review.

target.title: fix(ssh-tunnel): pin ensurePortAvailable to IPv4 loopback
target.body:
## Summary

Pass `"127.0.0.1"` to `ensurePortAvailable` in `startSshPortForward`
to match the actual SSH forward binding address family.

## Problem

The host-less `ensurePortAvailable(localPort)` probes the IPv6
wildcard on dual-stack hosts, missing IPv4-only occupants and
reporting a busy port as free.  Same root cause as #94379/#94394.

## Root Cause

`startSshPortForward` calls `ensurePortAvailable(localPort)` without
an explicit host.  Every other probe in the module pins to IPv4:
`pickEphemeralPort("127.0.0.1")`, `canConnectLocal("127.0.0.1")`,
and the forward itself binds `127.0.0.1`.

## Changes

- `src/infra/ssh-tunnel.ts` — add `"127.0.0.1"` as the second argument
  to `ensurePortAvailable`, matching #94394's fix pattern

## Related

Closes #94596

target.title: fix(telegram): drop unsupported local markdown link hrefs in rich HTML (fixes #94117)
target.body: ## Summary - Fixes #94117. - Telegram `sendRichMessage` rejects rich payloads when a Markdown link href is a local or relative path, returning `RICH_MESSAGE_URL_INVALID` and dropping the entire final reply. - Skip emitting `` only for hrefs outside Telegram's supported inline-link set. Supported schemes — `http://`, `https://`, `tg://`, `mailto:`, `tel:`, and in-document `#anchor` — stay clickable; everything else (local/relative paths) degrades to the visible link label (or existing file-ref `` wrapping when applicable). This matches Telegram's full inline-link contract: `mailto:`, `tel:`, and in-document `#anchor` hrefs stay clickable; only local/relative paths degrade to visible text. ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** Telegram final replies containing Markdown links to local filesystem or relative paths fail delivery with `RICH_MESSAGE_URL_INVALID` instead of degrading unsupported hrefs to visible text, while supported link targets stay clickable. **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux (WSL2), driving the Telegram formatter and the rich `sendMessageTelegram` → `sendRichMessage` path with mocked Bot API calls (no live bot credentials in this environment). **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts node -e " const { markdownToTelegramRichHtml } = await import('./extensions/telegram/src/format.ts'); const cases = { local: '[scripts/yougile.py](/home/user/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/yougile.py#L41)', relative: '[config](./openclaw.json)', https: '[docs](https://example.com/docs)', tg: '[user](tg://user?id=123)', mailto: '[support](mailto:user@example.com)', tel: '[call](tel:+123456789)', anchor: '[back](#top)', }; for (const [k,v] of Object.entries(cases)) console.log(k+':', markdownToTelegramRichHtml(v)); " ``` **Evidence after fix:** Formatter regression (`format.test.ts`, 49 cases) and send-boundary regression (`send.test.ts`, including local-path and relative-path rich delivery cases) pass. Local/relative href output contains no rejected `` anchor; supported schemes still render anchors. **Observed result after fix:** ``` local: scripts/yougile.py relative: config https: docs tg: user mailto: support tel: call anchor: back ``` Send-layer checks (mocked `sendRichMessage` payload): - absolute local path: no ``; label preserved as `…`; supported `https` link kept - relative path: no ``; label preserved as plain text; supported `https` link kept **What was not tested:** Live Telegram Bot API roundtrip (no bot token in this environment). Proof covers formatter output and the rich send payload handed to `sendRichMessage`. ## Tests and validation - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts` — 181 passed. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? Yes Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No Highest-risk area: Telegram rich-message link rendering for non-http hrefs Mitigation: Only suppresses anchors for hrefs Telegram already rejects; labels remain visible and supported `http`/`https`/`tg`/`mailto`/`tel`/`#anchor` links are unchanged. AI-assisted: yes

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(discord): disable ws 8.21.0 receiver part limits for gateway sockets
target.body:
## Summary

ws 8.21.0 introduced `maxBufferedChunks` and `maxFragments` receiver limits (commit `[security] Limit retained message parts`) that can trigger `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and close connections with code 1008 under certain traffic patterns.

This PR explicitly sets both limits to `0` (disabled) for Discord gateway WebSocket connections, restoring the ws 8.20.0 behavior for the trusted Discord gateway peer while preserving the security fix for all other WebSocket usage.

## Changes

- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.ts`**: Base class `createWebSocket()` now passes `maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0` with a `ws.ClientOptions` cast (since `@types/ws` 8.18.1 does not yet expose these fields).
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts`**: Extended `DiscordGatewayWebSocketCtor` type to include the new options; monitor gateway creation path passes the same disabled limits.
- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts`**: Added test verifying base class WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.test.ts`**: Added test verifying monitor plugin WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.proxy.test.ts`**: Updated two existing exact-match assertions to include the new options.

## Why `0` instead of raising the limits

- ws 8.21.0 treats `0` as disabled (`receiver.js:61`, `receiver.js:98`: `if (this._maxBufferedChunks > 0 && ...)`), matching the pre-8.21.0 behavior exactly.
- Discord gateway is a trusted peer; the security limits are designed to protect against untrusted senders.
- Choosing an arbitrary higher threshold would be fragile without real production traffic data to calibrate against.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ws 8.21.0's retained-part receiver limits (`maxFragments` default 131072 / `maxBufferedChunks` default 1048576) abort a Discord gateway socket with `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` / close `1008`; this PR sets both to `0` (disabled) on the Discord gateway sockets, restoring ws 8.20.0 behavior for that trusted peer.
- **Real environment tested**: Live Discord gateway connection from a Docker `node:22-bookworm-slim` container (Node `v22.22.3`) with the repo-pinned `ws@8.21.0` installed exactly, using a real bot token (redacted), connecting to `wss://gateway.discord.gg/?v=10&encoding=json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: build a `node:22-bookworm-slim` image with `npm install ws@8.21.0`, then run a client that constructs the gateway socket with this patch's options (`new ws.WebSocket(url, { handshakeTimeout: 30000, maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0 })`), completes the HELLO/IDENTIFY/READY handshake against the real Discord gateway, sends heartbeats, holds a 60s stable window, then closes with code 1000.
- **Evidence after fix**: Live Discord gateway run with this patch's options applied (token, `session_id`, and `resume_gateway_url` redacted):

```
[env] node=v22.22.3 ws=8.21.0
[discord] connecting wss://gateway.discord.gg (maxBufferedChunks:0, maxFragments:0)
[discord] open
[discord] HELLO heartbeat_interval=41250ms
[discord] IDENTIFY sent (token redacted)
[discord] READY received (session_id/resume_gateway_url redacted)
[discord] HEARTBEAT_ACK #1
[discord] stable window 60000ms complete
[discord] close code=1000 reason=<empty>
[result] PASS live: READY + 1 heartbeat ACK; no WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS during stable window
```

Supplemental deterministic mechanism repro against a local ws 8.21.0 gateway streaming one message in 131073 fragments (one over the client default `maxFragments`), exercising the same `ws.WebSocket` receiver path:

```
# default ws.WebSocket (ws 8.21 defaults)
[before] open
[before] error code=WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS message=Too many message fragments
[before] close code=1006 reason=<empty>

# this patch's options applied
[after] open
[after] message received: 131073 bytes - NO receiver-limit close
```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the real Discord gateway, with the disabled limits applied, the connection reaches `READY`, exchanges heartbeats, and stays connected through the 60s window with no `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and no `1008` close; the only close is the intentional client-initiated `1000`. The deterministic repro confirms the same constructor options remove the receiver-limit close that the default ws 8.21 client hits.
- **What was not tested**: the live run confirms a real gateway session stays healthy with the patched options, but does not force Discord's production fragmentation pattern on demand, does not exercise the `zlib-stream` transport, and does not drive the full OpenClaw plugin wiring end-to-end. The deterministic repro proves the ws 8.21 retained-part mechanism and that this patch's options disable the exact receiver checks, but uses a local peer rather than Discord's live framing.

## Tests

- `gateway.test.ts`: 25 passed
- `gateway-plugin.test.ts`: 17 passed  
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: 21 passed
- TypeScript check (tsgo): clean

Fixes #88330

target.title: fix(cli): avoid false local gateway unreachable on probe timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable
- add regression coverage for the #73535 split: default local probe false-negative while `--require-rpc` or direct `status` RPC succeeds

## Scope
This intentionally excludes unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation from #64400, which is routed to central security triage. The fix should stay in the ordinary status/probe classification and fallback path.

## Credit
Fixes the non-security path tracked in #48360. Uses reproduction evidence from #62762 and #73535, with historical evidence from #51357 and #42019. Thanks @RacecarGuy, @justinschille, @DJBlackhawk, @tianyaqpzm, and @0xrsydn.

## Validation
- `pnpm check:changed`

ProjectClownfish replacement details:
- Cluster: ghcrawl-156625-autonomous-smoke
- Source PRs: none
- Credit: Preserve reporter credit for #48360 by RacecarGuy, #62762 by justinschille, and #73535 by DJBlackhawk in the PR body.; Mention #51357 by tianyaqpzm and #42019 by 0xrsydn as earlier closed reports that supplied useful historical evidence.; Do not claim or implement #64400's unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation; say that security-shaped sub-scope is routed separately.
- Validation: pnpm check:changed

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451
